description:
## Goal

Consider whether `RefreshLoop::RefreshHealthMonitor` and
`RefreshLoop::HealthReporter` should be merged, and act on
the decision.

## Why now

- They share the same `Log.for("quickheadlines.feed")`
  namespace
- They both read/write the same status data
  (`RefreshHealthMonitor.status` is read by `HealthReporter`)
- The reporter is essentially a periodic log of the
  monitor's status
- Both have test coverage now (the monitor through
  Supervisor's spec; the reporter through its own spec)
- Both are tiny (the monitor is ~60 lines, the reporter is
  ~95 lines)

## Two valid options

**A. Merge into `HealthReporter`** — the monitor's lifecycle
methods (`record_cycle_start`, `record_cycle_complete`,
`record_failure`, `feed_fetch_started`,
`feed_fetch_completed`, `reset_failures`, `stuck?`,
`attempt_recovery`, `status`) all move into HealthReporter.
The reporter's `start` method stays as-is.

Pro: one module owns the entire health-and-memory
monitoring concern. Clear cohesion.

Con: HealthReporter grows. The supervisor's
`record_cycle_start` / `record_cycle_complete` /
`record_failure` calls are currently direct; merging means
they become `HealthReporter.record_cycle_start` etc., which
is a long name. Consider renaming the merged module to
`Health` or `Monitoring` to avoid the awkwardness.

**B. Keep separate, but tighten the relationship** — add a
doc comment on HealthReporter pointing at the monitor, and
vice versa. The reporter already calls
`RefreshHealthMonitor.status`, so the relationship is
documented at the call site.

Pro: low risk, no API churn. `RefreshHealthMonitor` is
re-exported at the top level for backward compat (the
admin_controller and api_base_controller use it).

Con: the duplication concern remains unaddressed.

## Decision needed from the user

This is a real design call. The ticket captures the
tradeoff but does NOT pick a side — the implementing agent
should ask the user which option they prefer before
committing to either path.

## Acceptance

- The decision is recorded in a comment on the epic
  (or on a follow-up ticket)
- If A: the merge is implemented, the new module is
  named, the admin_controller / api_base_controller call
  sites are updated, the spec for the merged module
  replaces the two separate specs, the alias is removed
  if all callers are updated
- If B: a comment in each module points at the other
- Full suite passes
- `just nix-build` succeeds
title: Merge RefreshHealthMonitor into HealthReporter
